free_text: Projectile fragmentation of radioactive beams of
68Ni, 69Cu, and 72Zn
Experiment performed at the National
Superconducting Cyclotron Laboratory (NSCL) at
Michigan State University using fragment separator
A1900 and high-resolution magnetic spectrometer S800
High acceptance magnetic spectrometer
consisting of two dipole and several correction
magnets with folowing detectors in focal plane
pair of cathode readout drift chambers
located about 1 m apart followed by a
multisegmented ion chamber and
four large focal-plane plastic scintillators
of thicknesses of 3 mm, 5 cm, 10 cm, and 20 cm,
respectively, with active area of 60 x 30 cm2.
A 131 MeV/A primary beam of 76Ge was accelerated and
struck a 245 mg/cm2 thick beryllium production
target. The average intensity of the 76Ge beam
during the experiment was about 200 electrical
nanoAmpere corresponding to 6.3E+10 pps. Secondary
beams of 64Ni, 68Ni, 69Cu, and 72Zn ions were
selected with the A1900 fragment separator.
376 mg/cm2 thick beryllium foil, placed inside the
scattering chamber in the S800 beam line
Total uncertainty including contributions
- detection (transport) efficiency
- flux normalization
Data corrected for beasm line missalignment
Compiled by S.H.

sent by author (S.L.)
